graph.name: Safety Incident Response: SINC-001
graph.description: Full safety incident response: safety triage -> diagnose -> contain -> remediate -> regulatory report -> lessons learned

safety-incident.start.context: Assess the severity, immediate danger, and nature of the safety incident. If there is immediate danger to personnel, prioritize evacuation and first aid before proceeding.

objective.hint: Brief description of the safety incident

severity.hint: How severe is this safety incident?

immediate_danger.hint: Is there immediate danger to personnel?

active_impact.hint: Is there ongoing impact right now?

area_secured.hint: Has the affected area been secured?

safety-incident.observe.context: Gather all observable facts about the problem. Do not jump to conclusions.

affected_systems.hint: What systems or components are affected?

safety-incident.hypothesize.prompt: Form a hypothesis about the root cause.
safety-incident.hypothesize.context: Based on your observations, what do you think is causing the problem?

hypothesis.hint: Your best explanation for the observed symptoms

safety-incident.test.context: Design and execute a test that would confirm or refute your hypothesis.

safety-incident.contain.context: Take immediate action to stop ongoing damage or impact.

safety-incident.remediate.context: Apply a fix for the root cause identified during diagnosis.

rollback_plan.hint: How to roll back if the fix fails?

safety-incident.regulatory_report.context: Determine if this incident is reportable under applicable regulations (e.g., OSHA, EPA, local authorities). Complete and file all required reports within mandatory timeframes.

reportable.hint: Is this incident reportable to regulators?

safety-incident.lessons_learned.prompt: Document lessons learned from this safety incident.
safety-incident.lessons_learned.context: Capture what went wrong, what went right, and what changes should be made to prevent recurrence. This feeds into the organization's continuous improvement process.

training_description.hint: What training is needed, if any?

safety-incident.conclude.context: Based on all evidence gathered, what is the root cause?

safety-incident.verify.context: Confirm the incident is fully resolved, the area is safe, and all affected personnel have received appropriate care.

area_safe.hint: Is the affected area safe to re-enter?

personnel_cleared.hint: Have all affected personnel been cleared?

safety-incident.observe.symptoms: Approximately 2L of concentrated sulfuric acid spilled from a cracked flask onto the bench surface. Fumes present. Minor splash burn on technician's forearm.
safety-incident.observe.affected_systems: Lab bench B-204-3, fume hood ventilation, one personnel (Tech J. Smith)

safety-incident.hypothesize.hypothesis: Flask had a pre-existing stress fracture from thermal cycling. The crack propagated during handling, causing sudden failure and acid release.
safety-incident.hypothesize.alternative_hypotheses: Mechanical impact during setup; defective glassware from supplier; improper storage causing stress

safety-incident.test.test_description: Visual inspection of remaining flask fragments under magnification. Checked thermal cycling log for flask batch B-2024-117. Reviewed supplier QC records.
safety-incident.test.test_result: Stress fracture pattern consistent with thermal fatigue found on fragment edges. Flask batch had 47 autoclave cycles, exceeding recommended 30-cycle limit.

safety-incident.contain.containment_action: Acid neutralized with sodium bicarbonate, area decontaminated per SOP-CHEM-003, bench surface replaced. Remaining flasks from batch B-2024-117 quarantined.

safety-incident.remediate.fix_description: Implemented 30-cycle retirement limit for all borosilicate glassware. Updated glassware tracking log to include cycle counts. Replaced all flasks exceeding limit.
safety-incident.remediate.rollback_plan: Revert to previous glassware inventory if replacement flasks show quality issues

safety-incident.lessons_learned.what_went_wrong: Glassware was used beyond its safe thermal cycling limit. No tracking system existed for cycle counts. Manufacturer's recommended limits were not codified in SOPs.
safety-incident.lessons_learned.what_went_right: Technician followed emergency spill protocol correctly. Area was secured within 2 minutes. First aid was administered promptly. Fume hood contained vapors.
safety-incident.lessons_learned.process_changes: 1) Implement glassware lifecycle tracking with cycle count limits. 2) Add visual age indicators (colored dots) to flasks. 3) Update SOP-CHEM-001 to include glassware inspection before use.
safety-incident.lessons_learned.training_description: All lab personnel to receive refresher on glassware inspection and lifecycle limits. New hires to include this in onboarding module.

safety-incident.conclude.root_cause: Borosilicate flask failure due to thermal fatigue from exceeding manufacturer's recommended 30-cycle autoclave limit (flask had 47 cycles). No lifecycle tracking existed.
safety-incident.conclude.evidence_summary: Stress fracture patterns on fragments consistent with thermal fatigue. Autoclave log confirms 47 cycles. Manufacturer spec sheet states 30-cycle limit. No SOP requirement for cycle tracking.
safety-incident.conclude.recommended_action: Implement glassware lifecycle tracking system, retire batch B-2024-117, update SOPs, conduct personnel training on glassware inspection procedures.

safety-incident.verify.verification_notes: Lab B-204 cleared for normal operations. Tech J. Smith returned to duty after first-aid treatment. All corrective actions implemented and verified.

safety-incident.close.summary: Chemical spill in Lab B-204 caused by thermal fatigue failure of borosilicate flask exceeding manufacturer cycle limits. One first-aid injury treated on-site. Root cause identified, corrective actions implemented (lifecycle tracking, SOP updates, training), OSHA report filed. Area restored to safe operations.

Root cause identified, corrective actions implemented (lifecycle tracking, SOP updates, training), OSHA report filed. Area restored to safe operations.
